briefing.opening: Good morning. Welcome to the climate briefing for June 3, 2026. Today's news primarily focused on warnings about impending climate threats, alongside various domestic and international efforts to address them.

sections.title: 🔴 Impending El Niño and Heatwave Threats
sections.content: Recently, warnings about a powerful global El Niño phenomenon and the resulting extreme heatwave risks have continued [12, 62, 77]. The UN has urged emergency action, expressing concern that heatwaves and floods will intensify due to climate change and El Niño. The World Meteorological Organization (WMO) announced an 80% probability of El Niño occurring between June and August, signaling a global climate disruption emergency [13, 14, 17]. Domestically, climate change has shifted the primary cause of national park access restrictions from cold waves to heatwaves, with Bukhansan National Park, in particular, facing a potential 82-day restriction [6, 9]. Furthermore, an emergency has been declared on the disease prevention front as mosquitoes are now remembering repellent scents as food [10]. The Ministry of Health and Welfare announced plans to strengthen welfare checks for vulnerable populations and suspend outdoor activities for elderly workers when a severe heatwave warning is issued [60]. The hidden risk of certain chemicals used in cooling systems contributing to global warming also raises awareness [15].

sections.title: 🌱 Climate Change Adaptation and Carbon Neutrality Efforts
sections.content: Despite the threats of climate change, various adaptation and mitigation efforts are actively underway both domestically and internationally. In Jeongeup City, Jeollabuk-do, 'Dalhami,' a customized new rice variety developed to cope with climate change, saw its first rice planting [1, 11]. Cases of growing lettuce and basil that can withstand heatwaves in urban gardens in Seoul have also been reported [5]. Cheongju City is responding to abnormal weather by significantly reducing the failure rate of rice seedbeds from 30% to 3% through 'timely sowing and transplanting' farming methods [18]. In the education sector, carbon neutrality and climate crisis response education is actively being conducted for elementary school students in Jeungpyeong-gun [2, 16, 24, 27, 28]. In Yeongcheon, the Hyanggyo and Yeongcheon City have launched an academy for responding to abnormal climate change, initiating practical education that connects tradition and environment [3, 4, 7, 8]. In addition, diverse carbon neutrality efforts continue, including Gwangmyeong City's green transition initiatives [19], Daegu Agricultural Technology Center's carbon-neutral lifestyle gardens [20], Hyundai Department Store's adoption of recycled paper shopping bags [61], and the development of technology to convert CO2 into stone [73].

sections.title: 🌍 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Industry Efforts
sections.content: The transition to renewable energy to achieve carbon neutrality goals is accelerating across domestic industries. Notably, the Export-Import Bank of Korea (KEXIM) is building a 'three-party win-win model' to strengthen ESG capabilities by supporting consulting costs for Samsung Electronics and its Vietnamese local partners' renewable energy transition and assisting with joint procurement [31, 33, 34, 35, 38, 39, 41, 42, 43, 44, 45, 47, 48, 50, 51, 53, 56, 57, 58]. Samsung's Vietnam factory has already begun directly purchasing renewable energy [32]. In domestic industrial complexes, the Daegu Regional Environmental Office is accelerating the renewable energy transition by expanding solar power generation [37, 40, 46, 54]. Efforts at the local community level are also prominent, with a surge in private solar power business permits for new and renewable energy in Cheonan City [55]. Furthermore, STOFF2 is designing a 100% renewable energy era by combining solar power and hydrogen [49]. The G7 Torino Ministerial Meeting adopted the Venaria Declaration, agreeing to expand renewable energy and phase out coal, demonstrating international cooperation [52].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Education and Technological Innovation for Carbon Neutrality
sections.content: Efforts in education and technological innovation to achieve carbon neutrality goals are also ongoing. The Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy and KOTRA have released educational videos and manuals for corporate practitioners, accelerating the achievement of carbon neutrality goals through international cooperation [29]. In the domestic K-bio sector, future technologies and strategies for solving food problems and achieving carbon neutrality are being fully mobilized, raising expectations [30]. However, the aviation industry has received a carbon-neutral bill in the form of mandatory Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF), and the speed of its implementation remains a challenge [22]. A multifaceted approach is crucial to overcome these challenges and move towards a sustainable future.

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlighted warnings about climate threats, as well as various domestic and international adaptation and mitigation efforts to counter them. Sustained attention and action to address climate change are more crucial than ever. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.title: “Elders, Have You Avoided the Heat?”... High-Risk Seniors to Receive Twice-Daily Check-ins During Severe Heatwave Warnings
articles.original_title: “어르신, 더위 피하셨어요?”···폭염중대경보 땐 하루 두 번 고위험 어르신 안부 묻는다
articles.description: The Ministry of Health and Welfare announced the implementation of the '2026 Summer Protection Measures for Vulnerable Groups.' Under these measures, if a 'severe heatwave warning' is issued this summer, welfare checks for vulnerable individuals, including elderly living alone, will be increased to twice daily. Additionally, outdoor activities for elderly employment programs will be completely suspended during the heatwave.

summary.phenomenon: The Ministry of Health and Welfare announced on the 3rd that it will implement the 2026 Summer Protection Measures for Vulnerable Groups. Accordingly, when a severe heatwave warning is issued this summer, welfare checks for vulnerable groups, including elderly living alone, will be strengthened to twice daily, and outdoor activities for elderly employment programs will be completely suspended.

articles.original_description: 보건복지부 제공올여름 최상위 폭염특보 단계인 ‘폭염중대경보’가 발령되면 독거 어르신 등 취약계층에 대한 안부 확인이 하루 2회로 강화된다. 무더위 속 노인 일자리 야외활동 역시 전면 중단된다.보건복지부는 3일 이 같은 내용을 담은 ‘2026년 여름철 취약계층 보호 대책’을 추진한다고 밝혔다. 정부는 지난 1일부터 폭염경보 수준인 지역에서 일 최고 체감온도 ···

articles.title: Hyundai Department Store's Eco-Friendly Shopping Bags Saved 53,000 Trees
articles.original_title: “나무 5만 3000그루 살린 현대백화점의 친환경 쇼핑백”
articles.description: Hyundai Department Store has reduced wood consumption by approximately 8,000 tons within four years of introducing its 'eco-friendly shopping bags,' an initiative equivalent to protecting 53,000 trees. According to the department store, in June 2022, it became the first in the industry to introduce 100% recycled paper shopping bags, made from waste paper, across all its nationwide stores. By the end of May this year, a total of 32 million such eco-friendly shopping bags have been produced.

summary.phenomenon: From June 2022 to the end of May 2024, Hyundai Department Store introduced 32 million 100% recycled paper shopping bags across its nationwide stores, resulting in an approximate 8,000-ton reduction in wood consumption over four years, an effect equivalent to protecting 53,000 trees.
summary.cause: This is due to Hyundai Department Store being the first in the industry to introduce 100% recycled paper shopping bags made from waste paper.

articles.original_description: 현대백화점 제공현대백화점이 ‘친환경 쇼핑백’ 도입 4년만에 목재 사용량 약 8000t을 절감했다. 나무 5만 3000그루를 보호한 것과 같은 효과를 낸 셈이다.3일 현대백화점에 따르면 2022년 6월 업계 최초로 전국 점포에 폐지로 만든 100% 재생지 쇼핑백을 도입했다. 이후 올해 5월 말까지 총 3200만장의 친환경 쇼핑백을 제작했다.100% 재생지로 ···
